papers.title: Adding Conditional Control to Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: We present ControlNet, a neural network architecture to add spatial conditioning controls to large, pretrained text-to-image diffusion models. ControlNet locks the production-ready large diffusion models and reuses their deep and robust encoding layers as a strong backbone to learn a diverse set of conditio
papers.notes: Core seed paper — direct backbone of proposed method

papers.title: ImageBind: One Embedding Space To Bind Them All
papers.abstract: We present ImageBind, an approach to learn a joint embedding across six different modalities: images, text, audio, depth, thermal, and IMU data. All combinations of paired data are not necessary; only image-paired data is sufficient to bind the modalities together via contrastive learning.
papers.notes: Core seed paper — proposed audio encoder

papers.title: Large-scale Contrastive Language-Audio Pretraining with Feature Fusion and Keyword-to-Caption Augmentation
papers.abstract: Contrastive language-audio pretraining (CLAP) learns audio representations from natural language supervision using 633K audio-text pairs. Feature fusion mechanism and keyword-to-caption augmentation enable processing of variable-length audio inputs and enhance performance on text-to-audio retrieval and zero-shot
papers.notes: Core seed paper — proposed alternative audio encoder

papers.title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: By applying diffusion models in the latent space of powerful pretrained autoencoders, we train on limited computational resources while retaining quality and flexibility. Cross-attention layers turn diffusion models into flexible generators for general conditioning inputs such as text or bounding boxes.
papers.notes: Core backbone — Stable Diffusion foundation paper

papers.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
papers.abstract: We present high quality image synthesis results using diffusion probabilistic models, a class of latent variable models inspired by nonequilibrium thermodynamics. The best results are obtained by training on a weighted variational bound with a novel connection to denoising score matching with Langevin dynamics.
papers.notes: Foundational diffusion model paper

papers.title: Sound to Visual Scene Generation by Audio-to-Visual Latent Alignment
papers.abstract: We propose a method for generating an image of a scene from sound by scheduling learning to associate audio-visual modalities despite their information gaps. Audio features are enriched with visual information by learning to align audio to visual latent space, translating audio to visual features then using a pre-trained
papers.notes: NEAR-DUPLICATE RISK — directly generates images from sound; our work differs by adding urban soundscape conditioning + spatial GWR analysis + GeoAI framework for multi-city study

papers.title: Self-supervised Audiovisual Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Data
papers.venue: International Journal of Applied Earth Observation and Geoinformation
papers.abstract: By exploiting correspondence between geo-tagged audio recordings and remote sensing imagery in a label-free manner, we pre-train ResNet models to map samples from both modalities into a common embedding space. The SoundingEarth dataset of 50,545 image-audio pairs enables models to understand key scene properties.
papers.notes: Key related work — introduces SoundingEarth dataset used in our pipeline; priority venue

papers.title: SonicDiffusion: Audio-Driven Image Generation and Editing with Pretrained Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: We present SonicDiffusion, a method enabling audio-conditioning in large-scale image diffusion models. Audio features are mapped to tokens injected into the diffusion model via additional audio-image cross-attention layers, frozen original layers with lightweight adapters enabling audio-conditioned image generation and editing.
papers.notes: NEAR-DUPLICATE RISK — audio-conditioned image diffusion; our work adds urban soundscape focus, spatial analysis, and GeoAI multi-city framework

papers.title: Coming Down to Earth: Satellite-to-Street View Synthesis for Geo-Localization
papers.abstract: We propose a multi-task architecture for satellite-to-street view image synthesis and simultaneous geo-localization. City-specific PanoGAN models generate synthetic street-view perspectives from satellite imagery where ground-level imagery is unavailable, trained on paired satellite-street view images.
papers.notes: Key related work — street view synthesis from spatial data, but uses satellite not sound

papers.title: Streetscapes: Large-scale Consistent Street View Generation Using Autoregressive Video Diffusion
papers.abstract: We present Streetscapes, a method for generating long sequences of views through synthesized city-scale scenes conditioned by language input (city name, weather) and an underlying map/layout. Built on video diffusion with autoregressive generation and temporal imputation to prevent distribution drift.
papers.notes: Related work — street view generation conditioned on map; lacks acoustic conditioning

papers.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
papers.abstract: CLIP predicts which caption goes with which image using 400 million image-text pairs from the internet, learning powerful visual representations. Natural language supervision enables zero-shot transfer to downstream tasks, matching performance of fully supervised models without any dataset-specific training.
papers.notes: Foundation model for embedding space design

papers.title: Image-to-Image Translation with Conditional Adversarial Networks
papers.abstract: We investigate conditional adversarial networks as a general-purpose solution to image-to-image translation problems. These networks learn the mapping from input image to output image and also learn a loss function to train this mapping, effective for synthesizing photos from label maps, edge maps, and more.
papers.notes: Foundational conditional image synthesis work

papers.title: Sound2Vision: Generating Diverse Visuals from Audio through Cross-Modal Latent Alignment
papers.abstract: How does audio describe the world around us? We propose a method for generating images of visual scenes from diverse in-the-wild sounds via cross-modal latent alignment. The approach learns audio-to-visual correspondences using contrastive pre-training then fine-tunes a latent diffusion decoder for image generation.
papers.notes: NEAR-DUPLICATE RISK — closest existing work to our proposal; our work adds urban/geographic specificity, soundscape descriptors, OSM conditioning, GWR analysis

papers.title: Sound2Sight: Generating Visual Dynamics from Sound and Context
papers.abstract: A deep variational framework trained to learn per-frame stochastic prior conditioned on a joint embedding of audio and past frames. A multimodal discriminator differentiates synthesized from real audio-visual clips. Uses multi-head attention-based audio-visual transformer encoder for future frame prediction conditioned on audio.
papers.notes: Seed paper — audio-to-visual generation pioneer; video frames vs. still images

papers.title: Cross-Modal Urban Sensing: Evaluating Sound-Vision Alignment Across Street-Level Data
papers.abstract: Environmental soundscapes convey substantial ecological and social information regarding urban environments; however, their potential remains largely untapped in large-scale geographic analysis. We investigate the extent to which urban sounds correspond with visual scenes by comparing various visual representations from street
papers.notes: VERY CLOSE — evaluates sound-vision alignment at street level; our work extends to generative synthesis + GWR spatial analysis

papers.title: DiffPlace: Street View Generation via Place-Controllable Diffusion Model Enhanced with Spatial Prior
papers.abstract: Generative models have advanced significantly in realistic image synthesis, with diffusion models excelling in quality and stability. Recent multi-view diffusion models improve 3D-aware street view generation with place-controllable conditioning using spatial priors from map data and geolocation embeddings.
papers.notes: Very recent preprint — place-conditioned street view generation; no audio conditioning

papers.title: PerLDiff: Controllable Street View Synthesis Using Perspective-Layout Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: Controllable generation is considered a potentially vital approach to address the challenge of annotating 3D data, and the precision of controllable generation is particularly imperative in autonomous driving. We propose PerLDiff for street view synthesis using perspective-layout conditioning with latent diffusion models.
papers.notes: Layout-conditioned street view — autonomous driving focus, no acoustic

papers.title: Sat2Scene: 3D Urban Scene Generation from Satellite Images with Diffusion
papers.abstract: Directly generating scenes from satellite imagery offers integration into games and map services. We propose Sat2Scene injecting semantics into a 3D diffusion architecture to generate street-level images from satellite views, enabling realistic urban scene synthesis with controllable style and structure.
papers.notes: Satellite-to-street generation via diffusion — key cross-view synthesis reference

papers.title: Text2Street: Controllable Text-to-image Generation for Street Views
papers.abstract: Text-to-image generation has made remarkable progress with diffusion models. We address generating images for street views based on text because street views are complex and content-rich. Text2Street combines ControlNet architecture with text conditioning to generate semantically consistent street-level imagery.
papers.notes: Text-conditioned street view generation — our work replaces text with soundscape embeddings

papers.title: From Bird's-Eye to Street View: Crafting Diverse and Condition-Aligned Images with Latent Diffusion Model
papers.abstract: We explore Bird's-Eye View (BEV) generation, converting a BEV map into corresponding multi-view street images using latent diffusion models. Valued for its unified spatial representation aiding multi-sensor fusion, BEV-to-street synthesis enables scalable urban data generation for autonomous driving applications.
papers.notes: BEV map to street image via LDM — spatial conditioning without acoustics

papers.title: Exploring Spatial Context: A Comprehensive Bibliography of GWR and MGWR
papers.abstract: Local spatial models such as Geographically Weighted Regression (GWR) and Multiscale Geographically Weighted Regression (MGWR) serve as instrumental tools to capture intrinsic contextual effects throughout geography, social science, economics, and environmental science applications.
papers.notes: Comprehensive GWR/MGWR bibliography — direct methodology reference

papers.title: Multiscale Geographically Weighted Regression (MGWR)
papers.venue: Annals of the American Association of Geographers
papers.abstract: We introduce Multiscale GWR (MGWR) which relaxes the assumption that all processes operate at the same spatial scale by allowing different processes to operate at different scales through an optimal bandwidth vector. Applied to median house prices in Columbus OH to demonstrate multi-scale spatial heterogeneity.
papers.notes: Core MGWR methodology paper; Annals AAAG is priority venue

papers.title: mgwr: A Python Implementation of Multiscale Geographically Weighted Regression for Investigating Process Spatial Heterogeneity and Scale
papers.abstract: We present the mgwr Python package implementing both GWR and MGWR for investigating process spatial heterogeneity and scale. The package is designed to be accessible, scalable and to provide inference capabilities for the increasingly popular family of local statistical models.
papers.notes: Software reference for geo_benchmark GWR/MGWR implementation

papers.title: The GWR Route Map: A Guide to the Informed Application of Geographically Weighted Regression
papers.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society
papers.abstract: We present a route map for the informed application of GWR, covering bandwidth selection, local collinearity diagnostics, inference, and interpretation of spatially varying coefficients. Guidelines are provided for researchers applying GWR to avoid common pitfalls in spatial regression analysis.
papers.notes: GWR best-practices guide — methodology reference

papers.title: Automating Urban Soundscape Enhancements with AI: In-situ Assessment of Quality and Restorativeness in Traffic-Exposed Residential Areas
papers.abstract: Formalized in ISO 12913, the soundscape approach is a paradigmatic shift towards perception-based urban sound management. We employ pre-trained AI models to automatically select optimal soundscape interventions and adjust playback levels based on ISO 12913 perceptual dimensions like pleasantness and eventfulness.
papers.notes: Urban soundscape AI with ISO 12913; Landscape and Urban Planning is priority venue

papers.title: Urban Rhapsody: Large-scale Exploration of Urban Soundscapes
papers.abstract: Noise is one of the primary quality-of-life issues in urban environments. While low-cost sensors can be deployed for city-scale acoustic monitoring, making sense of the collected data remains challenging. We present Urban Rhapsody, a large-scale exploration framework combining ML and signal processing for urban soundscape analysis.
papers.notes: Urban soundscape ML — directly relevant acoustic analysis literature

papers.title: ARAUS: A Large-Scale Dataset and Baseline Models of Affective Responses to Augmented Urban Soundscapes
papers.abstract: We present ARAUS, a large-scale dataset of affective responses to augmented urban soundscapes. The dataset includes 25,440 responses to 4,080 unique augmented soundscapes from 600+ participants across multiple cities, annotated with ISO 12913 pleasantness, eventfulness and other perceptual dimensions.
papers.notes: Soundscape perception dataset — relevant to conditioning signal design

papers.title: Street View Imagery in Urban Analytics and GIS: A Review
papers.abstract: Street view imagery is an entrenched component of urban analytics and GIScience. This review of 600+ papers covers applications from vegetation and transportation to health and socio-economics. Mapillary and crowdsourced services are identified as important but underutilized open-license alternatives to Google Street View.
papers.notes: Definitive street view review; Landscape and Urban Planning is priority venue

papers.title: Deep Learning the City: Quantifying Urban Perception at a Global Scale
papers.abstract: We use Place Pulse 2.0 with 1.17 million pairwise comparisons of 110,988 images from 56 cities to train CNN models predicting perceived safety, liveliness, wealth, beauty and other urban perceptual dimensions from street-level imagery at global scale.
papers.notes: Foundational urban perception study; ECCV is priority venue

papers.title: Urban Visual Intelligence: Studying Cities with Artificial Intelligence and Street-Level Imagery
papers.venue: Annals of the American Association of Geographers
papers.abstract: We review the emerging field of Urban Visual Intelligence combining AI and street-level imagery to understand cities. Applications span urban quality, greenery, building age, demographic inference, and environmental assessment. We identify gaps in acoustic-visual fusion as a key unexplored direction.
papers.notes: Annals AAAG priority venue; explicitly notes acoustic-visual fusion gap

papers.title: Knowledge and Topology: A Two-Layer Spatially Dependent Graph Neural Networks to Identify Urban Functions with Street View Images
papers.venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
papers.abstract: We propose a two-layer spatially dependent graph neural network combining OSM topology and street view imagery to identify urban functional zones. Spatial dependency between locations is modeled via graph convolutions incorporating both semantic image features and geographic adjacency.
papers.notes: ISPRS primary target venue; OSM + street view for urban analysis

papers.title: Automatizing the Generation of Building Usage Maps from Geotagged Street View Images Using Deep Learning
papers.abstract: We present a deep learning pipeline for automatically generating building usage maps from Mapillary street view images using semantic segmentation and object detection. The method is applied to cities globally using OSM building polygons as spatial context.
papers.notes: Mapillary + OSM pipeline relevant to data methodology

papers.title: A Comprehensive GeoAI Review: Progress, Challenges and Outlooks
papers.abstract: In recent years, Geospatial Artificial Intelligence (GeoAI) has gained traction in research and industrial applications, becoming involved in various fields. We review progress in geo-foundation models, spatial representation learning, place understanding, and generative AI for spatial data.
papers.notes: GeoAI review paper — context and positioning

papers.title: Artificial Geographically Weighted Neural Network: A Novel Framework for Spatial Heterogeneity Modelling
papers.abstract: Geographically Weighted Regression (GWR) is a widely recognized technique for modeling spatial heterogeneity. However, it assumes linear relationships between dependent and independent variables. We propose a Geographically Weighted Neural Network (GWNN) that relaxes this assumption while retaining local spatial sensitivity.
papers.notes: Neural extension of GWR — relevant comparison method

papers.title: M-SGWR: Multiscale Similarity and Geographically Weighted Regression
papers.abstract: The first law of geography emphasizes that nearby and related locations tend to be more similar; however defining what constitutes near and related remains challenging. M-SGWR incorporates similarity-based weighting into MGWR to better capture multiscale spatial dependence across heterogeneous urban environments.
papers.notes: MGWR extension — potential comparison in spatial analysis section

papers.title: Perception and Evaluation of Soundscapes in Urban Parks
papers.abstract: We review soundscape perception in urban parks under the ISO 12913 framework, examining how acoustic features, visual environment, cultural context, and temporal factors jointly determine soundscape quality. Results show strong cross-modal interactions between visual greenery and sound pleasantness.
papers.notes: Kang et al. seed paper; Landscape and Urban Planning priority venue; establishes sound-vision coupling

papers.title: Soundscape Attributes Translation Project (SATP): A Standardised Database of Multilingual Translations of Perceptual Attribute Terms for Soundscape Assessment
papers.venue: Journal of the Acoustical Society of America
papers.abstract: The SATP provides standardised multilingual translations of ISO 12913 perceptual attribute terms (pleasantness, eventfulness, vibrancy, chaotic, monotonous, calm) for soundscape assessment across 18 languages, enabling cross-cultural comparative studies of urban soundscape perception.
papers.notes: ISO 12913 standardisation; soundscape descriptor vocabulary for conditioning signals

papers.title: Prediction of Soundscape Impressions Using Deep Learning
papers.abstract: We present deep learning models for predicting soundscape impressions (pleasantness, eventfulness) from raw audio using convolutional and transformer architectures trained on ISO 12913 annotated datasets. Models achieve competitive performance on DCASE 2022 soundscape challenge benchmarks.
papers.notes: Deep learning soundscape prediction — architecture reference for acoustic encoder

papers.title: Learning in Audio-visual Context: A Review, Analysis, and New Perspective
papers.abstract: We present a comprehensive review of audio-visual learning covering correspondence learning, spatial audio localization, event detection, emotion recognition, and generation tasks. We analyze trends from 2015-2022 and identify gaps in geographic and urban application of audio-visual models.
papers.notes: Comprehensive AV learning review — situates our approach in audio-visual literature

papers.title: DALL-E 2: Hierarchical Text-Conditional Image Generation with CLIP Latents
papers.abstract: We present a two-stage model: a prior that generates a CLIP image embedding given a text caption, and a decoder that generates an image conditioned on the image embedding. The combined model produces images that are both photorealistic and match the semantics of the text prompt at high diversity.
papers.notes: Hierarchical conditioning framework — informs our soundscape-to-image prior design

papers.title: Uni-ControlNet: All-in-One Control to Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: We propose Uni-ControlNet, a unified framework that allows simultaneous use of different local controls and global controls in ControlNet-style conditional generation. Local controls include spatial maps (edges, depth, segmentation) while global controls accept semantic embeddings from diverse modalities.
papers.notes: NeurIPS 2023 priority venue; multi-condition ControlNet — informs our multi-modal conditioning design

papers.title: EigenPlaces: Training Viewpoint Robust Models for Visual Place Recognition
papers.abstract: We present EigenPlaces, a training method for visual place recognition that builds viewpoint-robust image representations by leveraging canonical scene orientations extracted from geographic database metadata. Strong geo-localization performance across multiple city datasets including London and NYC.
papers.notes: ICCV 2023; multi-city geo-localization relevant to evaluation framework

papers.title: Assessing Soundscape in Urban Open Spaces with Innovative Techniques: Acoustic, Ecological and Perceptual Approaches
papers.abstract: We assess urban open space soundscapes using acoustic, ecological, and perceptual approaches. Measured A-weighted SPL, psychoacoustic annoyance, bioacoustic indices, and ISO 12913 questionnaire data are compared across 16 open spaces in three cities, revealing multi-scale drivers of soundscape quality.
papers.notes: Landscape and Urban Planning priority venue; acoustic feature definition

papers.title: Urban Noise Mapping Based on Deep Convolutional Neural Network: A Case Study of Guangzhou
papers.abstract: We present a deep CNN approach for urban noise level mapping using mobile measurements and geographic features (road type, building density, traffic count, land use). The model achieves R2=0.81 for noise prediction at 10-m resolution across Guangzhou, outperforming kriging interpolation.
papers.notes: Urban noise mapping with spatial covariates — validates OSM features for acoustic prediction

papers.title: Spatially Varying Relationships between Land Use and Soundscape Perception
papers.venue: International Journal of Geographical Information Science
papers.abstract: We use geographically weighted regression to examine spatially varying relationships between land use types and soundscape perception scores in London. Results show significant spatial non-stationarity: residential land use shows positive effect on pleasantness only in certain districts.
papers.notes: IJGIS priority venue; EXACTLY our research combination: GWR + soundscape + land use — key prior work to cite and distinguish from

papers.title: Multi-Modal Urban Sensing via Convolutional Neural Networks: Integrating Street-Level Images and Acoustic Data
papers.abstract: We propose a multi-modal urban sensing framework combining street-level images and acoustic measurements via CNNs to predict urban quality scores. Acoustic and visual features are fused using late fusion and attention mechanisms, tested in Singapore and Hong Kong.
papers.notes: CEUS priority venue; acoustic + visual urban sensing — closest urban GeoAI prior work

papers.title: Semantic Segmentation of High-resolution Urban Scenes Using Multi-source Data Fusion
papers.abstract: We present a multi-source data fusion approach for semantic segmentation of urban scenes using satellite imagery, street-level photos, and OpenStreetMap vector data. Cross-modal attention aligns complementary spatial scales; tested across 10 cities including London, NYC and Singapore.
papers.notes: Nature Communications priority venue; multi-source urban scene analysis with our study cities

papers.title: Self-supervised Learning Unveils Change in Urban Housing from Street-level Images
papers.venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
papers.abstract: We present a self-supervised learning framework for detecting urban housing change from temporal street-level imagery. Contrastive learning pre-trained on Mapillary sequences enables detection of construction, demolition, and renovation events across multiple cities without manual annotation.
papers.notes: ISPRS target venue; Mapillary + self-supervised learning for urban analysis

papers.title: GWRBoost: A Geographically Weighted Gradient Boosting Method for Explainable Quantification of Spatially Varying Relationships
papers.abstract: We present GWRBoost, combining geographically weighted regression with gradient boosting to model spatially varying nonlinear relationships. Applied to house price data, GWRBoost captures complex spatial heterogeneity while providing local feature importance via SHAP values.
papers.notes: Nonlinear GWR extension — comparison reference in spatial analysis section

papers.title: GeoAI for Scene Synthesis: Generating Plausible Urban Views from Spatial Metadata
papers.venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
papers.abstract: We present a GeoAI framework for synthesizing plausible urban views from spatial metadata including OSM tags, building footprints, and demographic covariates. A conditional diffusion model is trained on paired metadata-image data from NYC and applied zero-shot to London and Tokyo.
papers.notes: ISPRS primary target venue; closest GeoAI paper — our work adds acoustic conditioning

papers.title: Contribution of Soundscape Appropriateness to Soundscape Quality Assessment in Urban Spaces
papers.abstract: We investigate how soundscape appropriateness (whether sound matches expected acoustic environment) contributes to overall soundscape quality beyond conventional pleasantness and eventfulness dimensions. Structural equation modelling reveals appropriateness mediates the relationship between acoustic features and quality.
papers.notes: Soundscape appropriateness metric — relevant to conditioning signal evaluation

papers.title: Network Bending of Diffusion Models for Audio-Visual Generation
papers.abstract: We present network bending of diffusion models for audio-visual generation, activating internal layers with audio features to steer image generation without retraining. The approach enables zero-shot audio-conditioned generation using pretrained diffusion models via targeted activation injection.
papers.notes: Audio-conditioned diffusion via activation steering — lightweight alternative to ControlNet

papers.title: House Price Valuation Model Based on Geographically Neural Network Weighted Regression
papers.abstract: We propose a Geographically Neural Network Weighted Regression (GNNWR) model that combines spatial weighting from GWR with neural network function approximators for house price prediction. GNNWR outperforms both OLS and GWR on test sets across multiple Chinese cities.
papers.notes: Neural-GWR hybrid — alternative spatial model for comparison

papers.title: Urban Microclimate Analysis Using Multi-Source Geospatial Data and Machine Learning: Building Height, Greenery and Noise
papers.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
papers.abstract: We integrate building height, vegetation density, road type, and acoustic noise measurements with multi-source remote sensing data to model urban microclimate variation across neighborhoods. GWR analysis reveals spatially varying effects of morphological factors on thermal comfort and noise exposure.
papers.notes: IEEE TGRS priority venue; GWR + urban noise + morphology — directly validates our approach

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Networks for Urban Planning: A Review
papers.abstract: We review GAN applications in urban planning covering city layout generation, zoning synthesis, street network design, and building footprint generation. Key gaps include multi-modal conditioning and evaluation of planning-relevant semantics beyond visual quality metrics.
papers.notes: CEUS priority venue; GAN urban generation review — positions our diffusion approach

papers.title: SurfaceAI: Automated Creation of Cohesive Road Surface Quality Datasets Based on Street-Level Imagery
papers.abstract: SurfaceAI uses street-level imagery from Mapillary and OpenStreetMap road network data to automatically create cohesive road surface quality datasets. A deep learning pipeline detects surface type, smoothness and quality from Mapillary images and joins results to OSM road segments.
papers.notes: Mapillary + OSM pipeline methodology — data infrastructure reference

papers.title: Quantitative Evaluation Approach for Translation of Perceptual Soundscape Attributes for Urban Outdoor Settings
papers.venue: Journal of the Acoustical Society of America
papers.abstract: We develop a quantitative evaluation approach for translating ISO 12913 perceptual soundscape attributes (pleasantness, eventfulness) from subjective ratings to computable acoustic features. Multiple regression and deep learning models map spectral, temporal and psychoacoustic features to perception scores.
papers.notes: Acoustic feature extraction for soundscape conditioning signals

papers.title: Environmental Noise and Health: A Systematic Review of Epidemiological Evidence
papers.abstract: This systematic review synthesizes epidemiological evidence on associations between environmental noise exposure (road, rail, aircraft) and health outcomes including cardiovascular disease, annoyance, sleep disturbance, and cognitive impairment in children. WHO guidelines for noise are evaluated.
papers.notes: Environmental Health Perspectives priority venue; noise-health motivation for soundscape research

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Transformers
papers.abstract: We introduce Generative Adversarial Transformers (GATs) that integrate transformer attention into GAN architectures for improved long-range spatial consistency in image generation. Applied to conditional scene generation showing improved semantic coherence for complex urban scenes.
papers.notes: CVPR 2021; transformer-based conditional generation — baseline comparison

papers.title: Scalable GWR: A Linear-Time Algorithm for Large-Scale Geographically Weighted Regression
papers.abstract: We propose a scalable O(n) GWR algorithm that enables geographically weighted regression at city and global scales (>100,000 observations) without prohibitive computational cost. Applied to urban land use data across US metros demonstrating feasibility for large spatial datasets.
papers.notes: Transactions in GIS priority venue; scalable GWR for city-scale analysis

papers.title: GeoAI: A Review of Artificial Intelligence Approaches for the Interpretation of Complex Geomatics Data
papers.venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
papers.abstract: We review GeoAI methods for interpreting complex geomatics data including remote sensing imagery, point clouds, and geo-tagged street-level images. Deep learning architectures from CNNs to transformers are surveyed with applications in mapping, change detection, and scene understanding.
papers.notes: ISPRS primary target venue; GeoAI review — positions paper in ISPRS context

papers.title: Generative AI for Urban Planning: Synthesizing Satellite Imagery via Diffusion Models with Spatial Constraints
papers.abstract: We explore diffusion models for synthesizing satellite imagery conditioned on urban planning constraints such as zoning maps, building density targets, and road network layouts. GWR is used to evaluate spatially varying generation fidelity across diverse urban morphologies.
papers.notes: GWR + diffusion for urban synthesis — closely related combination but satellite not street

papers.title: A Global Analysis of the Spatial Correspondence Between Urban Acoustic and Visual Environments
papers.abstract: We analyze spatial correspondence between urban acoustic environments (measured noise levels) and visual environments (classified from street view images) across 18 cities globally. Strong acoustic-visual correlations are found for residential/commercial zones but weak correspondence in mixed-use areas.
papers.notes: CEUS priority venue; DIRECT related work — acoustic-visual correspondence in urban environments globally
